encounter.reason: 4-year well-child examination, asthma step-up evaluation, immunizations
encounter.location: Triangle Pediatrics, 4201 Lake Boone Trail, Suite 200, Raleigh, NC 27607

conditions.name: moderate persistent asthma
conditions.note: Reclassified from mild persistent to moderate persistent based on symptom frequency: daytime symptoms 3–4 times/week, nighttime symptoms 2–3 times/month, rescue inhaler use ~3 times/week

conditions.name: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis

medications.name: fluticasone propionate 44 mcg inhaler
medications.reason: moderate persistent asthma — discontinued as monotherapy insufficient

medications.name: fluticasone/salmeterol 45 mcg/21 mcg inhaler (Advair HFA 45/21)
medications.sig: 1 puff inhaled twice daily via spacer with mouthpiece
medications.reason: moderate persistent asthma — step-up from low-dose ICS monotherapy to medium-dose ICS/LABA combination

medications.name: montelukast 4 mg chewable tablet
medications.reason: moderate persistent asthma — add-on controller therapy

medications.name: albuterol 90 mcg inhaler
medications.sig: 2 puffs inhaled as needed for wheezing or shortness of breath via spacer

medications.name: cetirizine 5 mg oral tablet
medications.note: Dose increased from 2.5 mg to 5 mg daily based on current weight and ongoing symptoms

medications.name: epinephrine auto-injector 0.15 mg (EpiPen Jr) two-pack
medications.reason: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis; tree nut allergy

medications.name: triamcinolone 0.1% ointment
medications.sig: apply to eczema flares twice daily as needed

immunizations.name: DTaP vaccine (diphtheria, tetanus, acellular pertussis), 5th dose

document_references.description: Comprehensive 4-year well-child visit note documenting subjective history of suboptimal asthma control, physical examination findings, developmental screening, vision and hearing screening results, immunization administration, asthma reclassification to moderate persistent with therapy step-up plan, and anticipatory guidance. 35-minute visit with >50% counseling/coordination.

Revised asthma action plan reflecting step-up to fluticasone/salmeterol 45/21 with spacer mouthpiece, addition of montelukast, continued albuterol PRN rescue therapy, and updated green/yellow/red zone criteria.
